2606.24781.title: Assessing Distribution Shift in Human Activity Recognition for Domain Generalization
2606.24781.paper_summary: While the field of Human Activity Recognition (HAR) continues to draw interest from researchers and advance in important ways, some key challenges remain. One of the most difficult aspects of building HAR models that show good performance in real-world settings is dealing with data diversity from device and sensor heterogeneity, and contextual changes that are intrinsic to real-world applications. While data diversity in HAR has been well-acknowledged in the literature, there remains a gap in understanding the effect of various types of distribution shifts on HAR models and the domain generalization problem that arises. Towards that end, this paper systematically evaluates 4 different types of distribution shifts, including variations in device type, sensor placement, sampling rate, and user behavior. Quantifying their effects, we illustrate that diversity shifts predominantly define all types of shifts, indicating the existence of unique features that are not shared across different domains. We then introduce a uniform HAR-based distribution shift benchmarks and conduct a comprehensive evaluation of up to 28 domain generalization methods. Our analysis exposes the limitations of current domain generalization algorithms in achieving model generalizability, marginally outperforming the empirical risk minimization baseline. This work represents the first systematic exploration of domain generalization and adaptation concerning specific distribution shifts in sensor-based HAR, offering an open-source benchmark platform and datasets to spur further research.
2606.24781.paper_summary_zh: 雖然人類活動識別（HAR）領域持續吸引研究者的興趣並在重要方面取得進展，但仍然存在一些關鍵挑戰。建立在現實世界環境中表現良好的 HAR 模型的最困難方面之一是處理來自設備和傳感器異質性的數據多樣性，以及固有於現實世界應用的上下文變化。雖然文獻中已經很好地認識到 HAR 中的數據多樣性，但對於各種類型的分佈變化對 HAR 模型的影響以及由此產生的領域泛化問題的理解仍然存在差距。為此，本文系統地評估了 4 種不同類型的分佈變化，包括設備類型、傳感器放置、採樣率和用戶行為的變化。通過量化其影響，我們展示了多樣性變化主要定義了所有類型的變化，表明存在不在不同領域之間共享的獨特特徵。然後，我們引入了一個基於 HAR 的統一分佈變化基準，並對多達 28 種領域泛化方法進行了全面評估。我們的分析揭示了當前領域泛化算法在實現模型泛化性方面的局限性，僅僅略微超越了經驗風險最小化基準。這項工作代表了對於基於傳感器的 HAR 中特定分佈變化的領域泛化和適應的首次系統性探索，提供了一個開源基準平台和數據集，以促進進一步的研究。
2606.24781.author: Rebecca Adaimi et.al.
